myth: I can vote anywhere in the country.
fact: In many systems, you must vote at your assigned polling station.
explanation: Polling stations are usually linked to your registered address or constituency. Rules vary, but you should confirm your assigned location.
whatToDoTip: Verify your polling station in the voter list and plan your route in advance.

myth: I need only a voter ID card.
fact: Some regions accept multiple forms of approved identification.
explanation: Accepted IDs can vary. Some places require a specific voter card; others allow alternates.
whatToDoTip: Check your region’s accepted ID list and carry a backup if allowed.

myth: If I am 18, I can vote automatically.
fact: Turning 18 does not always register you automatically.
explanation: Many systems require registration or a verified voter record before you can vote.
whatToDoTip: Register early (if required) and verify your name in the voter list.

myth: If my name is not on the voter list, I can still vote.
fact: In many regions, your name must appear on the official voter list to vote.
explanation: Polling staff often verify your details against an official roll. If you are missing, you may need to resolve it before polling day.
whatToDoTip: Use the correction window to fix missing/incorrect details and re-check closer to polling day.
